expectations.text: Writes failure record to harness/trace/failures/ in correct JSON format
expectations.evidence: Both memory.md and completion.md format failure records shown, written to harness/trace/failures/

expectations.text: Failure JSON includes: timestamp, task, error_type, error_message, attempted_fixes (3 items), resolved, context_turns_at_failure
expectations.evidence: completion.md format includes all required fields including context_turns_at_failure: 53. memory.md format has resolution but lacks context_turns_at_failure.

expectations.text: Checks context budget at each repair attempt
expectations.evidence: Context budget check shown after each attempt with turns >= 40 threshold evaluation

expectations.text: Records context_turns_at_failure value in failure JSON
Escalates to human after 3 failed attempts
expectations.evidence: BLOCKER report shown with 'Manual intervention required' and 3 repair attempts detailed

expectations.text: Notes that this failure reveals missing lint mapping → candidate for Critic analysis
expectations.evidence: Yes, flagged for Critic. All 3 conditions met: same error 2+ times, required human intervention, reveals missing lint rule

expectations.text: Failure JSON format is consistent between memory.md and completion.md specs (has context_turns_at_failure)
expectations.evidence: FORMAT INCONSISTENCY CONFIRMED: memory.md has 'resolution' field but lacks 'context_turns_at_failure'; completion.md has 'context_turns_at_failure' but lacks 'resolution'. Both define the same record type with different schemas. Output correctly identifies this as a problem in Question 6.
